request.prompt: Create one classroom-safe educational image about How Plants Make Food: Photosynthesis. Subject: Science (086). Grade level: Class X / 10. Topic focus: Photosynthesis Mechanism. Must show visually: Process: Photosynthesis; Raw Materials: Carbon Dioxide + Water + Sunlight; Product: Glucose (Food) + Oxygen; Location: Leaves containing Chlorophyll. Visual goal: Clean, labeled diagram of a leaf cross-section. Show arrows entering from bottom (roots/water), top/sides (air/CO2), and sun rays hitting the surface. Inside the leaf cells, show chloroplasts as green ovals capturing light energy.. Teaching context: Explain the inputs and outputs of photosynthesis clearly.. Student takeaway: Students understand that plants use sunlight, water, and CO2 to make food (glucose) and release oxygen.. Show the scientific or educational concept itself, not a presentation slide about the concept. Do not dr...
